examples.name: 1. an exact path
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/health
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/health
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: 2. a wildcard segment (*)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/*/health
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/health
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: 3. everything below (**)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/**
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/users/123
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: 4. capture a segment, check a trait
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
    methods:
      - GET
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha
            - beta

examples.name: 5. the whole model in one tree
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  # allows /api/v4/projects/g%2fp/issues
  # denies /api/v4/projects/g%2fp/secret
  - |-
    path.match(literal("api/v4/projects",
        capture_encoded("project", set("/"),
            greedy_without("secret"))),
    allow_encoded(set("/")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/mygroup%2fmyproject/issues
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: exact
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/health
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/health
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: multiple exact
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/health
      - /api/v4/status
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/status
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: a percent in a plain literal is a load error
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # A plain literal pins exact bytes, so a
  # "%" in it is a dead rule: a request token
  # carrying an encoded char never matches a
  # plain literal. Reject it at load and
  # steer the author to encoded_literal or
  # the {name:/} sugar, which match by
  # decoded value.
  - paths:
      - /a%2Fb
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: single segment *
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/*/health
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/health
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: greedy **
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/**
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/users/123
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: multiple methods
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/**
    methods:
      - GET
      - HEAD
examples.input:
request:
    method: HEAD
    path: /api/users
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: wrong method denied
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/**
    methods:
      - GET
examples.input:
request:
    method: POST
    path: /api/users
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: CONNECT is not a routable method, so listing it is a load error
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # CONNECT targets an authority, not a
  # slash-path, so the tokenizer rejects a
  # CONNECT request and a rule listing it
  # could only ever be a dead rule. Reject
  # it at load instead.
  - paths:
      - /api/**
    methods:
      - CONNECT
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: checked against a set
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /data/*/{letter}/**
    where: contains(set("a", "b", "c"), vars.letter)
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /data/x/b/info
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: checked against a trait
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
    methods:
      - GET
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha
            - beta

examples.name: case-insensitive capture check with lower
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # lower folds the captured project so the
  # trait check matches regardless of case,
  # so "Alpha" and "alpha" both qualify.
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], lower(vars.project))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/Alpha/issues
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha
            - beta

examples.name: equals identity
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/user/{username}
    where: user.name == vars.username
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/user/alice
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: slash: a trailing slash is required
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /files/
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: optional: match with or without a trailing slash
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  - |-
    path.match(literal("files",
        optional(slash())))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: optional: an optional trailing subtree
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  # The path may end at files, or carry
  # the optional reports tail, from one
  # tree with no duplicated prefix.
  - |-
    path.match(literal("files",
        optional(literal("reports"))))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: path, method, and trait
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /teams/{team}/**
    methods:
      - GET
    where: contains(user.traits["teams"], vars.team)
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /teams/security/dashboards
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        teams:
            - security
            - devs

examples.name: additive list (two rules)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /public/**
  - paths:
      - /static/**
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /static/css/app.css
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: carve-out (paths grant, negated subtree)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  # Allow all of /files, then carve out the
  # /files/secret subtree with a negated
  # path.match. The grant and the carve-out
  # share one expression, since path matching
  # never lives in a sugared where.
  - |-
    path.match(literal("files", greedy())) &&
    !path.match(literal("files",
        literal("secret", greedy())))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/a
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: anti-pattern (commented out)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
      # - /api/status
      #   {project} not bound for this path
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha
            - beta

examples.name: carve-out: exclude one segment with !
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # !secret matches any one segment except
  # "secret", then ** matches the rest.
  # allows /files/report/q4
  # denies /files/secret/q4
  - paths:
      - /files/!secret/**
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/report/q4
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: glob_without: exclude single segments
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  - |-
    path.match(literal("files",
        glob_without(set("private", "secret"))))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/report
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: greedy_without: exclude and carve out subtrees
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  - |-
    path.match(literal("files",
        greedy_without("secret", "private")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/reports/q3
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: greedy_except: exact segment, subtree still allowed
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  # allows /files/secret/other
  # denies /files/secret
  - |-
    path.match(literal("files",
        greedy_except(literal("secret"))))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/secret/other
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: greedy_except: add greedy() to exclude the whole subtree
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  # same as greedy_without("secret"): the
  # greedy() excludes the whole subtree.
  # denies /files/secret/key
  # allows /files/public/x
  - |-
    path.match(literal("files",
        greedy_except(literal("secret", greedy()))))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/secret/key
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: greedy_except: re-allow a path inside a denied subtree
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  # denies the secret subtree but re-allows
  # secret/public nested inside it.
  # allows /files/secret/public/y
  # denies /files/secret/x
  - |-
    path.match(literal("files",
        greedy_except(literal("secret",
            greedy_except(literal("public", greedy()))))))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/secret/public/y
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: root: several first segments in one tree
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  - |-
    path.match(root(
        literal("api", greedy()),
        literal("admin", greedy())))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /admin/settings
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: root: a path under no root is denied
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  - |-
    path.match(root(
        literal("api", greedy()),
        literal("admin", greedy())))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /public/x
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: an encoded node without allow_encoded fails closed
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # {package:/} names an encoded segment;
  # without allow_encoded it fails closed.
  # denies /registry/@babel%2fcore
  # allows /registry/lodash
  - paths:
      - /registry/{package:/}
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /registry/@babel%2fcore
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: npm scoped package binds its decoded id
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # allow_encoded opts the match in.
  # {package:/} binds the decoded id, so
  # /@scope%2fname reads as @scope/name.
  # allows /registry/@babel%2fcore
  - paths:
      - /registry/{package:/}
    allow_encoded:
      - /
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /registry/@babel%2fcore
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: GitLab project id, plain or encoded, in one node
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # One {project:/} binds a plain id or an
  # encoded group/project in one node, and
  # ** matches the rest of the route.
  # allows project 123
  # allows project mygroup%2Fmyproject
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project:/}/**
    allow_encoded:
      - /
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/123/issues
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: encoded_literal matches one group/project by its decoded value
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  # encoded_literal matches one segment by
  # its decoded value, in either hex case
  # (%2F or %2f).
  # allows project mygroup%2Fmyproject
  # denies project other%2Fthing
  - |-
    path.match(literal("api/v4/projects",
        encoded_literal("mygroup/myproject", set("/"),
            literal("issues"))),
    allow_encoded(set("/")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/mygroup%2Fmyproject/issues
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: an encoded id, a carved-out subtree, and the encoded-slash opt-in
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources_expression:
  # allows /api/v4/projects/g%2fp/issues
  # denies /api/v4/projects/g%2fp/secret
  - |-
    path.match(literal("api/v4/projects",
        capture_encoded("project", set("/"),
            greedy_without("secret"))),
    allow_encoded(set("/")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/mygroup%2fmyproject/issues
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: allow code and reason
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
    allow_code: allowed_project
    allow_reason: User has access to project
    deny_code_hint: denied_project
    deny_reason_hint: User has NO access to project
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha
            - beta

examples.name: deny hint: path matched, where failed
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
    allow_code: allowed_project
    allow_reason: User has access to project
    # deny_code_hint is reported on the near-miss: the path and
    # method matched but the where failed.
    deny_code_hint: denied_project
    deny_reason_hint: User has NO access to project
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/gamma/issues
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha
            - beta

examples.name: deny hints across rules, only some fire
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
    deny_code_hint: denied_project
    deny_reason_hint: Not allowed on this project
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/admin/**
    where: contains(user.roles, "admin")
    deny_code_hint: denied_admin
    deny_reason_hint: Admin role required
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/gamma/issues
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha

examples.name: an allow_reason with no allow_code is a load error
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # A reason explains a code, so a reason
  # with no code has nothing to qualify and
  # would be silently dropped. Reject it at
  # load, the same as the deny side rejects a
  # reason hint with no code.
  - paths:
      - /api/**
    allow_reason: this endpoint is public
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: a deny_code_hint with no where is a load error
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # A deny hint explains a near-miss, when
  # the path and method matched but the where
  # did not. With no where there is no
  # near-miss to qualify, so the hint could
  # never fire. Reject it at load rather than
  # silently dropping it.
  - paths:
      - /api/**
    deny_code_hint: needs_mfa
    deny_reason_hint: this endpoint requires MFA
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: unsafe_allow_all grants every path and method
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # The escape hatch: grant the whole app,
  # any method, any path, even an encoded
  # slash mid-path that /** would reject.
  # allows GET /anything/at/all
  # allows a mid-path %2F
  - unsafe_allow_all: true
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /anything/at/all
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: unsafe_allow_all cannot be combined with another field
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # unsafe_allow_all is all-or-nothing, so
  # pairing it with paths (or any field) is
  # a load error rather than a silent ignore.
  - unsafe_allow_all: true
    paths:
      - /api/**
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: a present-but-empty paths list is a load error, not an open grant
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # An empty list is treated as unset, the
  # same as omitting paths, so clearing it
  # never silently grants unrestricted
  # access. Use unsafe_allow_all to ask for
  # that on purpose.
  - paths: []
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: no matching role
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
# The user must hold this role for
# its app_resources to apply.
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/api/v4/health"]
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/health
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [auditor]
